report_name: Government agencies hit by ransomware daily in H1 2026, study finds

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: Identified as the most prolific threat group targeting government organizations in the first half of 2026, with 22 documented claims.

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: Identified as one of the most prolific threat groups targeting government organizations in the first half of 2026, with 21 documented claims.

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: Identified as one of the most prolific threat groups targeting government organizations in the first half of 2026, with 14 documented claims.

threat_actors.name: APT73/BASHE
threat_actors.description: Identified as an active threat group targeting government organizations in the first half of 2026, with 12 documented claims.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as a common initial access vector, with campaigns targeting government employees.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Used as an initial access vector by exploiting unpatched public-facing services.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Used for initial access by leveraging stolen credentials purchased on the dark web.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: A core impact tactic used by ransomware groups to encrypt data across government networks.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Used to exfiltrate sensitive data for double extortion tactics.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers frequently attempt to disable recovery options, such as deleting volume shadow copies using vssadmin, to prevent restoration.

attack_patterns.name: OS Credential Dumping
attack_patterns.description: Credential dumping tools like Mimikatz are frequently used by ransomware groups to escalate privileges.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Trust Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware actors perform reconnaissance to enumerate domain controllers using commands like 'nltest /dclist:'.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: Ransomware used by the Qilin group in attacks against government organizations.

malware.name: LockBit
malware.description: Ransomware used by the LockBit group in attacks against government organizations.

tools.name: Mimikatz
tools.description: A credential dumping tool frequently used by ransomware groups to escalate privileges within compromised government networks.

identities.name: Government Organizations
identities.description: Government organizations (federal, state, and local) were targeted by ransomware attacks at an average rate of one per day in the first half of 2026.

indicators.name: Domain Controller Enumeration with nltest
indicators.pattern:
title: Domain Controller Enumeration with nltest
id: 2b3c1d4e-5f6a-4b9c-8d7e-6f5a4b3c2d1e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of nltest to enumerate domain controllers, a common reconnaissance technique for ransomware actors.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/government-ransomware-roundup-h1-2026-stats-on-attacks-ransoms-and-data-breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1482
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\nltest.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: '/dclist:'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Mimikatz Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Mimikatz Execution
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8a7b6c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Mimikatz, a credential dumping tool frequently used by ransomware groups.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/government-ransomware-roundup-h1-2026-stats-on-attacks-ransoms-and-data-breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    - Image|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
    - OriginalFileName: 'mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/government-ransomware-roundup-h1-2026-stats-on-attacks-ransoms-and-data-breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection

indicators.name: Mimikatz File IOC
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Mimikatz File Creation
id: 8f7e6d5c-4b3a-4c2d-1e0f-9a8b7c6d5e4f
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of a file named mimikatz.exe, a known credential dumping tool.
references:
  - https://www.comparitech.com/news/government-ransomware-roundup-h1-2026-stats-on-attacks-ransoms-and-data-breaches/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-17
tags:
  - attack.t1003
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\mimikatz.exe'
  condition: selection
